insight: Structure agent capabilities as discrete autonomy levels (L0-L5) progressing from human-supervised actions to exception-managed orchestration, enabling calibrated delegation of decision-making authority based on task scope and risk.
applies: Defines clear handoff points between human oversight and autonomous execution in spec-driven frameworks and social bots, preventing both under-delegation (bottlenecking) and over-delegation (uncontrolled drift).
effect: Operators can map each client requirement or bot behavior to a specific autonomy level, making delegation decisions explicit and testable rather than implicit, reducing both safety incidents and unnecessary human-in-loop friction.
why_improves: Removes ambiguity about when agents should act independently vs. escalate; creates measurable progression paths for capability expansion; aligns technical architecture with actual risk tolerance.

insight: Use industrial-grade tooling (automated refactoring, systematic code generation, design assistance) instead of manual craftsmanship; this scales quality and velocity by orders of magnitude (10x refactor efficiency, 5x MR quality/coverage).
applies: Spec-driven frameworks and autonomous bots generate boilerplate and orchestration logic; systematic tooling (linters, generators, design validators) replaces hand-coded variants and reduces review friction.
effect: Reduces manual refactoring cycles, increases test coverage consistency, accelerates MR throughput, and embeds design patterns into generation rather than review.
why_improves: Moves from ad-hoc prompt-chaining to reproducible, measurable pipelines; eliminates vibe-coding variance by enforcing structure at generation time, not polish time.
